root.narration: Your team is down 0-1 early in the game. The coach asks for your input: the other team is parking a lot of players in their defensive third and playing on the counter. Your team keeps getting stuck with no space in the middle. What formation adjustment would you suggest?

choices.text: Push the outside backs higher and go wider to stretch their packed defense. Use a 3-4-3 to overload the attack!
Add another striker up top and just lump long balls forward!
Keep the same formation and just try harder in the middle.

outcome.headline: Stretched Them Out! Goal!
outcome.explanation: By going wider and pushing your outside backs forward, you forced the other team to spread out and cover more of the field. When a team packs the middle, width is the answer! A 3-4-3 gives you more attackers and wingbacks who stretch the defense from sideline to sideline. Once they spread out, gaps appear in the middle.
outcome.whatToRemember: When the other team packs the middle, use WIDTH to stretch them. Push outside players higher and force them to cover the whole field!

keyTerms.definition: How your team lines up on the field (like 4-3-3 or 3-4-3). The numbers tell you how many defenders, midfielders, and forwards you have.
Using the full sideline-to-sideline space to stretch a compact defense.

outcome.headline: Long Balls Are Easy to Defend!
outcome.explanation: Adding another striker sounds aggressive, but if you just kick long balls at a packed defense, their tall center-backs will win every header. More strikers only help if you can get them the ball in space. You need width and movement to open up a packed defense, not just extra bodies in a crowded area.
outcome.whatToRemember: Adding attackers does not help if the ball cannot reach them in space. Create space FIRST with width and passing!

outcome.headline: Same Problems, Same Results!
outcome.explanation: Doing the same thing and hoping for a different result will not work. If the middle is packed, you need to change your approach. Tactics and formations exist to solve problems! When something is not working, a smart team adjusts their shape to exploit the other team's weaknesses.
outcome.whatToRemember: If your current approach is not working, change your formation or tactics! Smart teams adjust based on what the opponent is doing.
